I once watched two candidates give the exact same correct answer to an Angular question, and only one of them actually understood it.
The first recited a definition. The second explained what breaks when you get it wrong, and fixed it live, without hesitation.
We hired the second one. Not because of the answer. Because of what was underneath it.
These ten questions are the ones I now reach for in every interview I run, and honestly, the ones I quietly ask myself whenever I review my own code. Read through them slowly. If even three of these make you pause, that pause is worth more than any tutorial you will watch this month.
Question 1: What Actually Happens When You Add [ngModel] to a Component Without FormsModule?
Most candidates say “it just will not work.” That is true, but it is not the answer that shows understanding.
The real answer is that Angular’s template compiler does not recognize ngModel as a valid attribute at all if FormsModule was never imported, because directives have to be registered somewhere for the compiler to know they exist. The result is a compile-time error, not a silent runtime failure.
